title: After Kernel upgrade to 5.10 Podman bind volume mount stopped to work: Error: invalid container path...
body:
/kind bug
**Description**
Looks like regression due to Linux Kernel upgrade or bug in the new WSL Kernel
After upgrade the Microsoft WSL kernel to the latest 5.10 bind volume mount stopped to work. The error is  
podman run -v type=bind,ro,src=/mnt/d/Pavel,dst=/mnt/ docker.io/library/ubuntu
Error: invalid container path "type=bind,ro,src=/mnt/d/Pavel,dst=/mnt/", must be an absolute path
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. It comes with regular Windows release update :(
2. Pull Ubuntu image from Docker.io
3. podman run -v type=bind,ro,src=/mnt/d/Pavel,dst=/mnt/ docker.io/library/ubuntu

**Describe the results you received:**
Error: invalid container path "type=bind,src=/mnt/d/Pavel,dst=/mnt/dPavel", must be an absolute path

**Describe the results you expected:**
Container is running and host directory is mounted as at /mnt/dPavel like 1 week ago.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman --version
podman version 3.1.0
crun version 0.19
commit: e67a75672412975916dac6b87f8346f870e4b99a
spec: 1.0.0
+SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes/No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
WSL Kernel
Linux MSI-wsl 5.10.16.3-microsoft-standard-WSL2 #1 SMP Fri Apr 2 22:23:49 UTC 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
+systemd-genie

The container starts without mount. /mnt exists inside container owned by root.
The Fedoradistro works OK and

tmpfs             814868        0    814868   0% /run/user/0 This error is known from the Docker-for-win. Their, reset solves the problem but podman system reset doesn't.
The ability to create symlinks in the rootfs by the new kernel is not broken. Are there other pre-requisites for bind mount to test?

comments.body: Your podman command is incorrect, you have to use the --mount flag not --volume when you want to use this syntax, `podman run --mount type=bind,ro,src=/mnt/d/Pavel,dst=/mnt/ docker.io/library/ubuntu`

OK, SOLVED! I tried many things until found that in the WSL Kernel boot log that the new WSL Kernel supports FUSE. So in the storage.conf the line 
mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs" must be uncommented. The long hot discussion with WSL gave its fruits.
I suppose, for >= Kernel 5.10 and Fedora34 it should be the default.
